sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for node in seg['nodes']:
    if node['pixels'] >= 10 and node['color'] not in ('B', 'g', 'S'):
        b = node['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  color={node['color']}, pixels={node['pixels']}, center=({(min(rows)+max(rows))//2},{(min(cols)+max(cols))//2})")
w_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'w']
print(f"Light gray segments: {len(w_nodes)}")
for n in w_nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    print(f"  pixels={n['pixels']}, center=({(min(rows)+max(rows))//2},{(min(cols)+max(cols))//2})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
The gray diamond at (9,49) is now white (W, pixels=12). The gray diamond at (10,44) is now G (G, pixels=12) - it was white before! So clicking the gray diamond at (9,49) made it white and the previous white diamond at (10,44) became gray.
The orange square is still at (13,33). It seems like clicking the gray diamond made it white, but the orange square didn't move.
Now I see the pattern: clicking a gray diamond makes it white. The orange square moves toward the white diamond. But the orange square is at (13,33) and the white diamond is at (9,49).
Wait, but the orange square didn't move after I clicked the gray diamond at (9,49). Let me check if the orange square needs to be clicked to move, or if it moves automatically.
Actually, looking at the history, the orange square moved when I clicked the light gray path segments, not when I clicked the gray diamond. So the mechanic is:
1. Click a gray diamond to make it white (this sets the target).
2. Click light gray path segments to move the orange square toward the white diamond.
But I already clicked the gray diamond at (9,49) and it's now white. The orange square is at (13,33). Let me click the light gray segments to advance the orange square toward (9,49).
The light gray segments are:
- (11,40) - 4 pixels
- (12,37) - 3 pixels
- (14,29) - 2 pixels
- (15,27) - 3 pixels
- (16,24) - 3 pixels
- (17,20) - 3 pixels

The orange square is at (13,33). The white diamond is at (9,49). The segments toward (9,49) are (11,40) and (12,37).
Let me click (11,40) to advance the orange square.
Wait, but I've been doing this and the orange square just oscillates. Let me think about this differently.
Actually, looking at the image, I see the orange square is at (13,33) and there's a gray diamond at (10,44) and a white diamond at (9,49). The orange square is between the gray diamond and the white diamond.
Maybe the orange square needs to reach the white diamond at (9,49). Let me click the segments in order from closest to farthest from the orange square.
The orange square is at (13,33). The closest segment toward (9,49) is (12,37). Let me click it.
